topic.what: AgentFlow system for automatically synthesizing multi-agent harnesses for vulnerability discovery
topic.how: typed graph DSL jointly optimizes agent roles prompts tools communication topology and coordination protocols with runtime diagnostics feedback
topic.why_matters: hand-written harnesses span 4x performance range on same LLM; existing automated approaches search narrow slices of design space and rely on coarse pass/fail feedback

characteristics.context: TerminalBench-2 89 long-horizon terminal tasks with Claude Opus 4.6

characteristics.what_itasures: zero-day vulnerabilities discovered in Google Chrome
characteristics.evidence: confirmed by Chrome Vulnerability Reward Program
characteristics.context: Google Chrome codebase 35M+ lines C/C++ with Kimi K2.5

characteristics.what_it_measures: critical severity vulnerabilities including sandbox escapes
range of success rates across different harness designs
characteristics.evidence: three systems on same model tasks same 89 tasks
characteristics.context: demonstrates harness design impact on outcomes

good_for: finding memory-safety bugs in large codebases
systems needing multi-agent coordination for analysis

novelty: typed graph DSL unifying all harness dimensions into single searchable representation
runtime diagnostics feedback instead of coarse pass/fail signals
joint optimization across agents prompts tools communication and coordination
type system enabling tractable search over full design space
open_problems: scaling to even larger and more complex codebases
reducing compute budget required for synthesis
handling targets with limited or no instrumentation
optimizing for different vulnerability classes beyond memory-safety
